headline: Jordan blocked Swiss arms-inspection team — SECO report casts doubt on re-export compliance.
summary: A delegation dispatched by the State Secretariat for Economic Affairs (SECO) in February 2026 managed to verify the presence of only slightly more than a third of Swiss-origin materiel sold to Jordan — mainly rifles, pistols, and silencers.

headline: Israel arrests four Swiss nationals during Gaza flotilla interception off Cyprus.
summary: Israeli naval forces began boarding vessels in the Global Sumud Flotilla on 18 May; by Monday evening at least 39 of the 50+ ships that departed from Marmaris, Turkey, had been intercepted.

headline: Cantons — including Vaud — reject federal plan to convert Ukrainian S-protection to B residence permits.
summary: Several Swiss cantons have formally objected to the Federal Council's intention to transition beneficiaries of temporary protection status (S-permit) to ordinary B work permits after five years, arguing the change would transform the S-instrument from temporary protection into a de facto permanent r

headline: Swiss army continues field trials of minidrones as both ISR and precision-strike platforms.
summary: The Swiss Armed Forces' Taskforce Drones is running the latest round of minidrone trials, evaluating platforms for both reconnaissance and as precision weapons — the first formal Swiss military assessment of drones with a lethal-strike role.

headline: Bilaterals III package now in parliament; referendum window open, vote expected 2027.
summary: After the signing ceremony on 2 March 2026 between Federal President Karin Keller-Sutter and European Commission President Ursula von der Leyen, the Federal Council adopted the dispatch to parliament in March.

headline: Trump calls off planned Iran military strike — Gulf states' request and "serious negotiations" cited.
summary: President Donald Trump announced Monday 18 May that he had ordered the U.S.

headline: Israel intercepts Gaza-bound flotilla off Cyprus; 39+ vessels boarded, international condemnation follows.
summary: The Global Sumud Flotilla — more than 50 vessels from Marmaris, Turkey — was intercepted by Israeli naval forces beginning 18 May in international waters off Cyprus.

headline: Israel-Lebanon ceasefire extended 45 days; daily Israeli strikes in southern Lebanon continue.
summary: The 16 April 2026 ceasefire between Israel and Lebanon — part of the wider regional de-escalation linked to the 2026 Iran war — was extended for 45 days on 15 May, now running to at least late June.

headline: Ukraine-Russia: three-day ceasefire expired; talks remain stalled; Putin signals war "coming to an end."
summary: A U.S.-brokered three-day ceasefire (9–11 May) and prisoner exchange (1,000 per side) produced little durable progress.

headline: India-Pakistan ceasefire marks one-year anniversary — holding, but fragile.
summary: One year on from the May 2025 Operation Sindoor missile strikes and the subsequent 10 May 2025 ceasefire, India and Pakistan remain technically at peace.

headline: CVE-2026-42945 "NGINX Rift" actively exploited — CVSS 9.2, heap buffer overflow enabling possible unauthenticated RCE.
summary: A critical heap buffer overflow in `ngx_http_rewrite_module` affects NGINX Open Source versions 0.6.27 through 1.30.0 and NGINX Plus R32–R36 (pre-patch).

headline: CVE-2026-20182 — Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN authentication bypass (CVSS 10.0) on CISA KEV; exploited by UAT-8616.
summary: An unauthenticated remote attacker can bypass authentication and gain administrative privileges on Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Controller and Manager.

headline: CVE-2026-39987 — Marimo Python notebook pre-auth RCE (CVSS 9.3) exploited within 10 hours; NKAbuse botnet payload observed.
summary: The terminal WebSocket endpoint `/terminal/ws` in Marimo (all versions before 0.23.0) lacks authentication, allowing any network-accessible attacker to obtain a full PTY shell.

headline: SEPPmail Secure Email Gateway — four CVEs including CVSS-10.0 pre-auth RCE; InfoGuard Labs (CH) advisory published.
summary: Multiple critical vulnerabilities affect SEPPmail versions before 15.0.4, disclosed by Swiss security firm InfoGuard Labs: **CVE-2026-2743** (CVSS 10.0) — path traversal in the Large File Transfer (LFT) module allows arbitrary file write chained to full RCE by overwriting `/etc/syslog.conf`; **CVE-2

headline: Sparx Systems Pro Cloud Server — four CVEs including auth bypass and RCE, CERT Polska advisory published 19 May.
summary: CERT Polska published a coordinated advisory today covering multiple vulnerabilities in Sparx Systems Pro Cloud Server (all versions through 6.1): **CVE-2026-42096** — broken access control allowing any low-privilege user to run arbitrary SQL queries; **CVE-2026-42097** — authentication bypass by om

headline: Canvas/Instructure education platform breached by ShinyHunters — 275 M records, 9,000 institutions.
summary: The ShinyHunters ransomware/extortion group claims to have exfiltrated 3.65 TB of data from Instructure's Canvas LMS, affecting approximately 275 million students, teachers, and staff at nearly 9,000 educational institutions, including Harvard and Stanford.

headline: Stuttgart Landeshauptstadt (Germany) hit by Rhysida ransomware — 19 May.
summary: The city of Stuttgart, capital of Baden-Württemberg, confirmed a ransomware intrusion attributed to the Rhysida group on 19 May 2026.
